reviewer_objection: This looks like only five miscellaneous public-sector anecdotes, not a publishable result.
reviewer_safe_response: The publishable result is not a denominator claim but a bounded observability-method contribution: the five-row package shows four distinct public evidence states for institutional AI—project-page visibility, regulator-document visibility, register disclosure, and register-plus-family-level procurement continuity—with explicit claim ceilings.
forbidden_overread: Do not rewrite the panel as prevalence, completeness, or a stable cross-country sample.
remaining_gap: The package still needs one exact buyer-side or page-specific public-assessment companion to show a fully bridged row.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/results defense paragraph; reviewer-response memo; thesis chapter on evidence infrastructures.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the panel mechanism-safe and land one exact companion before widening rhetoric.

reviewer_objection: Amsterdam is already enough to prove procurement transparency because the public register names supplier and governance fields.
reviewer_safe_response: Amsterdam is the richest current exact-row register exemplar, but the tested local route set still does not expose a buyer-side tender, award, contract, or downloadable public companion document for the same row. Exact-row machine readability and buyer-side traceability remain analytically separate dimensions.
forbidden_overread: Do not caption Amsterdam as procurement-absent, contract-resolved, implementation-verified, or public-assessment-complete.
remaining_gap: Buyer-side/public-companion inspectability remains unresolved on tested public routes.
paper_thesis_use: Main-text contrast sentence; methods inset on exactness versus traceability.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one genuinely different static official procurement or public assessment route for Amsterdam.

reviewer_objection: Helsinki proves exact contract linkage, so the public-sector package already closes the procurement gap.
reviewer_safe_response: Helsinki is stronger than a register-only row because an official same-buyer procurement package and IBM-consistent award metadata are public, but the linkage remains family-level rather than exact page-specific proof for the parking-chatbot row.
forbidden_overread: Do not caption Helsinki as exact contract-linked procurement proof, page-specific award proof, or public-assessment completeness.
remaining_gap: No page-specific contract, tender-lot, DPIA, or impact-assessment document explicitly tied to the parking-chatbot row is locally attached.
paper_thesis_use: Results paragraph on middle-state linkage; thesis note on family-level versus row-specific support.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep Helsinki frozen unless a page-specific companion explicitly tied to the register row appears.

reviewer_objection: The two UK ICO rows make this a procurement/government-spend finding because regulator notices are authoritative.
reviewer_safe_response: The UK ICO rows are authoritative for narrow legal-basis, remedy, deployment-timing, consent, and DPIA/process-failure language. They are deliberately retained as governance-depth comparators, not as buyer-side procurement or supplier-trace rows.
forbidden_overread: Do not convert regulator notice text into procurement visibility, supplier proof, sector prevalence, or generalized legality conclusions beyond the ICO documents.
remaining_gap: No local buyer-side procurement, supplier chain, public contract, or downloadable public-assessment companion is attached.
paper_thesis_use: Authority-depth comparator note; reviewer-safe quotation control.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Upgrade only if a public buyer-side procurement, supplier, contract, or public-assessment companion emerges.

reviewer_objection: ORION should be dropped because it is too thin to matter.
reviewer_safe_response: ORION is kept as the explicit low-trace floor. It demonstrates a distinct official project-page visibility state where public-body involvement and project framing are visible but procurement, register, and public-companion layers remain unresolved; that floor is analytically useful because it prevents the package from pretending every public-sector row reaches document or register depth.
forbidden_overread: Do not restate ORION as procurement-backed deployment, legality proof, supplier-normalized reconstruction, or measured operational effectiveness.
remaining_gap: The row remains weak for procurement or technical reconstruction and belongs below default hero views.
paper_thesis_use: Methods appendix floor case; thesis explanation of minimum observability states.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep ORION as control/floor unless a procurement, register, or technical companion appears.

reviewer_objection: Table 1B still uses one transparency axis and hides the fact that these are incomparable surfaces.
reviewer_safe_response: The current package explicitly separates surface class, traceability rung, buyer-side trace state, exact-row machine readability, and public-companion state. Amsterdam-versus-Helsinki contrast already demonstrates that richer register exactness does not imply stronger buyer-side continuity.
forbidden_overread: Do not flatten the panel into one generic transparency score, maturity score, or procurement ranking.
remaining_gap: A reviewer may still want one more visually explicit sidecar tying likely objections to the existing Table 1B controls.
